description:
A tool for configuring and managing joystick devices.

Joystick Gremlin is a program that allows the configuration of joystick like devices, similar to what CH Control Manager and Thrustmaster's T.A.R.G.E.T. do for their respectively supported joysticks. However, Joystick Gremlin works with any device be it from different manufacturers or custom devices that appear as a joystick to Windows. Joystick Gremlin uses the virtual joysticks provided by vJoy to map physical to virtual inputs and apply various other transformations such as response curves to analogue axes. In addition to customizing joysticks, Joystick Gremlin also provides powerful macro functionalities, a flexible mode system, scripting using Python, and many other features.
New Features
- Show error message if Gremlin can't start due to system misconfiguration
- Show information and warnings about incorrect action or action sequence configurations
- Keyboard input tab added
- Visualization of action sequences on input buttons
Fixes
- Better handling of vJoy setups
- Improve QML object cleanup
- Device swap improvements
- Disable input highlighting when using modal windows
- Improve handling of invalid actions
- Device bar properly scrolls when more devices than space are present
- Calibrations no longer required to be centered around 0
- Fix logical device inputs not updating properly
- Action ordering now lives in the Actions tab of the options screen
- Correctly handle paths with special characters
